phishing_cta_count.detected_method: phishing CTA patterns (click here, verify now, etc)

obfuscated_links.detected_method: href vs visible text mismatch (paypal.com link → evil.com)

HIGH_severity_count.reasoning: obfuscated links, forms, javascript, credentials, auth failures, return-path mismatch

MEDIUM_severity_count.reasoning: urgency patterns, URL shortener, hidden elements, sender mismatch

phishing_cta_count.detected_method: no suspicious CTAs (View PR link is legitimate)

HIGH_severity_count.reasoning: Legitimate email should have no HIGH findings

MEDIUM_severity_count.reasoning: Legitimate email should have minimal findings

phishing_tp_detection.metric: TC_001 should be classified as HIGH or CRITICAL
phishing_tp_detection.reasoning: Multiple correlated indicators: urgency + CTA + credentials + auth failures + obfuscated links

phishing_tn_detection.metric: TC_002 should be classified as LOW
phishing_tn_detection.reasoning: All authentication headers pass, sender domain legitimate, no suspicious patterns

false_positive_risk.metric: Clean emails should not exceed MEDIUM score
false_positive_risk.reasoning: GitHub emails have legitimate links and no phishing indicators

malware_and_attachment_risk.note: Test samples do not contain attachments

expected_coverage.detection_method: declared vs actual MIME type checking
PDF stream analysis for embedded executables

tpr_tnr_estimation.reasoning: Authentication failures are highly correlated with spam/phishing; rarely false positive on legitimate mail

false_positive_rate.reasoning: Legitimate services pass authentication; false positives mainly from new/misconfigured senders

final_weighted_score.note: floor rules may push to 45-70 (HIGH) minimum
No floor rules apply (no suspicious indicators)

systematic_bias_detection.undercounting_scenario: If phishing sample scores <45, indicates that:
reasons: Body NLP classification disabled or failing
Floor rules not applying to multi-high indicators

risk_scoring_calibration.overcounting_scenario: If clean sample scores >20, indicates that:
reasons: False positives in header analysis (legitimate domain checks)
Over-aggressive URL risk scoring (GitHub is CDN, may trigger new domain checks)

nlp_classifier_performance.note: NLP classification from scikit-learn LogisticRegression + TF-IDF

evidence: CTA spam (click now, limited time, verify account)

false_positive_risk.legitimate_alerts: May be misclassified as phishing if they contain:
examples: Urgent action CTAs (e.g., password reset links)
Credential mentions in legitimate context (e.g., API token warnings)
false_positive_risk.mitigation: Score weighting: NLP confidence is combined with header/body context, not used alone

training_data_coverage.note: NLP model training data unknown; validation will test on generic phishing patterns
training_data_coverage.recommendation: Run confusion matrix on live validation set to measure precision/recall

tc_001_edge_cases.detection_method: domain_similarity check or WHOIS age check
tc_001_edge_cases.expected_detection: should flag as HIGH risk if similar to known brand
should flag as MEDIUM/HIGH (suspicious for legitimate services)

tc_001_edge_cases.detection_method: href vs visible text domain mismatch

tc_001_edge_cases.expected_detection: MEDIUM severity (legitimate but suspicious in phishing context)

known_underdetection_risks.mitigation: Header analysis + NLP classification should catch

known_underdetection_risks.risk: Domain similar but not identical to brand
known_underdetection_risks.mitigation: Requires fuzzy domain matching (not currently in scope)
No OCR — requires attachment/base64 image analysis

before_production.checkpoint: Check false positive rate on corpus

before_production.method: Verify AbuseIPDB/VirusTotal/crt.sh hits on malicious indicators

threshold_tuning.rationale: SPF/DKIM/DMARC failures are 99% correlated with phishing
threshold_tuning.action: Consider increasing header weight to 0.4 (from 0.35) if FP rate > 3%

threshold_tuning.rationale: Body text is strongest indicator after headers
threshold_tuning.action: Monitor NLP false positive rate; may need retraining on corpus-specific data

threshold_tuning.rationale: Current floor is 45 (HIGH boundary); consider 50+ for better HIGH/CRITICAL separation
threshold_tuning.action: Measure impact on clean email corpus before increasing

feature_enhancements.rationale: Typosquats and newly registered domains bypass current detection

feature_enhancements.feature: Brand name extraction and fuzzy matching
feature_enhancements.rationale: Detect PayPal impersonation even with minor typos
IP/URL hits from AbuseIPDB/VirusTotal should boost risk score

testing_expansion.test: Add corpus of 50+ legitimate emails (newsletters, alerts, transactional)
testing_expansion.purpose: Measure false positive rate and calibrate thresholds

testing_expansion.test: Add corpus of 50+ phishing emails (various brands, techniques)
testing_expansion.purpose: Measure true positive rate across diverse attack vectors

testing_expansion.test: Add corpus of 20+ malware-bearing emails (various attachment types)
Add corpus of 30+ spam emails (bulk sender, list emails)
testing_expansion.purpose: Measure spam detection TP rate and FP rate

Generate confidence scores for each detection category
